title: Add Jest tests covering CLI version, help, validation, and CRUD
acceptance_criteria: tests/cli.test.ts (or similar under tests/) covers: (1) 'note --version' and '-V' produce 'fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0' on stdout and exit 0; (2) 'note --help' and '-h' exit 0 and include each subcommand in usage; (3) empty/whitespace --title on create exits 1 with a stderr message and no stack trace; same for --id on read/update/delete; (4) CRUD subcommands hit the right HTTP endpoint and method (use a mocked fetch or a supertest-driven express stub) and exit 0 on success; (5) HTTP 4xx response causes non-zero exit and stderr message. npm test passes with the new tests added.
notes: review: CRUD tests fail on Windows — spawned CLI subprocess cannot connect to in-process Express stub. Fix: use jest.mock('../src/cli/http') to stub the request function and import action handlers in-process, OR keep spawnSync but bind stub server explicitly to 127.0.0.1 with fixed port. Avoid spawnSync timeout issues.

title: Implement CRUD subcommands: list, read, create, update, delete
acceptance_criteria: Subcommand modules under src/cli/commands/ (or wired inline) implement: list [--tag <tag>] [--q <query>] -> GET /notes (query params only added when provided); read --id <id> -> GET /notes/:id; create --title <title> [--content <content>] [--tags <tag,...>] -> POST /notes (tags split on comma, trimmed; content defaults to empty string if omitted); update --id <id> [--title] [--content] [--tags] -> PUT /notes/:id and requires at least one of --title/--content/--tags (else exit 1 with stderr message, no stack); delete --id <id> -> DELETE /notes/:id and prints a confirmation line to stdout; all successful commands print JSON (or confirmation) to stdout and exit 0; HTTP 4xx/5xx prints the error message to stderr and exits non-zero; empty/whitespace strings for required args are rejected via task 3's validation helper with exit 1 + stderr message.

title: Add HTTP client wrapper and CLI input validation helpers
acceptance_criteria: src/cli/http.ts exports a thin wrapper over global fetch that issues GET/POST/PUT/DELETE to the configured base URL, parses JSON responses, and on HTTP 4xx/5xx throws an Error whose message is the server-supplied error string (or status text fallback) — no stack traces leak to stderr; src/cli/validation.ts exports requireNonEmptyString(name, value) that throws a clear Error for empty/whitespace-only strings, used by --title/--content/--id; both modules are pure TS, no extra dependencies; small unit-tested behavior is exercised via the subcommand tests in task 5.

title: Scaffold CLI entrypoint with --version, --help, and global --base-url
acceptance_criteria: src/cli/index.ts exists with a #!/usr/bin/env node shebang and uses commander; package.json bin maps 'note' to dist/cli/index.js; tsconfig includes src/cli (already covered by src/**/*); commander is configured with program.exitOverride() (or equivalent) so CommanderError surfaces as a human-readable stderr message + exit 1, never a stack trace; 'note --version' and 'note -V' print exactly 'fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0' then exit 0 (covers gh-toy-4ef); 'note --help' and 'note -h' exit 0 and show usage listing all subcommands; each subcommand stub supports '--help'/'-h'; a global --base-url option exists and NOTE_API_URL env var is honored, defaulting to http://localhost:3000; after 'npm run build', 'node dist/cli/index.js --version' prints the version (add a small post-build step or include shebang in source — verify tsc preserves it for module:commonjs).

title: Install commander dependency and pin Node engine for fetch
acceptance_criteria: commander v10+ added to package.json dependencies and present in node_modules; package.json declares engines.node >=18 so the CLI can use the global fetch API without adding node-fetch; npm install completes cleanly; npm test still passes.

title: CLI CRUD commands (list/read/create/update/delete)
description: Implement five subcommands against the NoteAPI: list (optional --tag/--q filter), read (--id required), create (--title, --content required), update (--id required, --title/--content optional), delete (--id required). Each command calls the API and prints results to stdout. Non-zero exit on API error.

title: CLI help system and input validation
description: The CLI must support --help/-h flags globally and per subcommand, printing usage and exiting 0. Input validation must reject empty or whitespace-only arguments with a clear error message and non-zero exit code. No stack traces in error output.

title: Add --version flag to CLI
description: The CLI tool should support a --version (or -v) flag that prints the current version string and exits with code 0. Acceptance: running ./tool --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0, exit code 0, works alongside other flags.
